name: error-handling-gate
description: Verify errors are handled gracefully with meaningful user feedback. Issues result in WARNINGS.
capabilities: Check for empty catch blocks that silently swallow errors
Verify user-friendly error messages without technical jargon
Ensure loading states are properly cleaned up on failure
Validate error logging includes sufficient context for debugging
Confirm retry options and fallback behaviors are implemented
Review error differentiation for different failure types
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation file containing checklists and guidelines for code review. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access. All 27 static findings are false positives caused by pattern-matching without semantic understanding. The skill contains only markdown documentation and JSON metadata.
valueStatement: Code reviews often miss error handling issues that cause silent failures and poor user experience. This gate provides a structured checklist to ensure all code handles failures gracefully with helpful user feedback.
limitations: This is a documentation gate for human reviewers, not automated scanning
Does not execute or test any code directly
No access to user files or project repositories
Requires manual application by developers during code review

useCases.title: Systematic Error Review
useCases.description: Use structured questions and checklists to review error handling during pull request reviews.

useCases.title: Quality Gate Enforcement
useCases.description: Establish error handling standards and verify submissions meet quality thresholds.

useCases.title: Learn Error Patterns
useCases.description: Study common error handling mistakes and learn best practices from the examples provided.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code for error handling. Check for empty catch blocks, loading state cleanup, and user-friendly messages.

promptTemplates.title: User Feedback Review
promptTemplates.prompt: What error messages will users see? Are they actionable and free of technical jargon?

promptTemplates.title: Failure Scenario Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: What happens when network fails, validation fails, or third-party APIs return errors? Walk through each failure mode.

promptTemplates.title: Production Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: If something goes wrong in production, how would we debug it? Review error logging and context provided.

bestPractices: Always wrap async operations in try/catch with meaningful error context
Provide user-friendly error messages that explain what went wrong and suggest next steps
Use finally blocks to ensure loading states and resources are always cleaned up
antiPatterns: Empty catch blocks that silently swallow errors without any logging or user feedback
Exposing technical error messages like 'TypeError: Cannot read property of undefined' to users
Missing loading state cleanup leaving UI stuck in spinning state after failures

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to any language. Examples use JavaScript syntax but principles work everywhere.

faq.question: What severity levels does this gate use?
faq.answer: High severity for empty catch blocks, medium for loading state issues, low for generic messages.

faq.question: Can this integrate with automated CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: This is a documentation guide for human reviewers. Add these checks to your code review process.

faq.question: Does this skill access or modify any files?
faq.answer: No. This is a read-only documentation skill that provides guidelines for manual code review.

faq.question: What if our team has different error handling standards?
faq.answer: Customize the checklist and severity guide to match your team's coding standards and conventions.

faq.question: How does this compare to automated linting tools?
faq.answer: This gate catches conceptual issues that tools miss, like message quality and user experience concerns.
